The point is is that Once my team Find someone who's financially qualified They buy 90 percent of the time The challenge is is making sure that you don't spend one hour on the phone with someone who is simply not financially qualified And even though I've said this to my team many times I still see them do it again and again and again. It's a tendency All right, so the question really is is and this is a very common thing So when you look at your business and you'll just look at the reality of what you do How does it play out at the end of the day? Why are most people not buying from you when you get someone on the phone? I'm just going I'm getting over being sick, right? So when you get someone on the phone and you make a presentation What percentage of the time? Is it money that's causing them to say I just I'm sorry. It all sounds great. I love this. I can't afford it Is it high? Is it 70 is it 60 70 percent? And that's a very different thing from I'm not liquid right now It's kind of not a great time for me that I love that's a qualified lead There's a very big difference from someone saying I'm not really liquid right now Things aren't that great. So someone's saying I'm broke I'm from Bangladesh You know, I'm from I'm from a part of I'm from New Delhi, India My you know annual income is is $2,000. I have no credit card and you're there trying to sell them a $2,000 product Or a $300 product That's what we call a waste of time And as a salesperson Time is probably your most valuable commodity. How many times a day it's not how many times a day you get off pitches How many times a day do you get off relevant pitches? There's a very big difference between the two How much time are you spending speaking to people that no matter how great you sound? No matter how perfect your solution is They can't buy from you because they can't afford it Be honest If that number Is over 30 percent of the time You've got to change your approach right now You've got to figure out a way to get to that truth Before you enter a full blown sales presentation Because the amount of time you're wasting is it's toxic Not only is it toxic, but what happens is you start thinking there's something wrong with you. Maybe I'm not closing right Maybe I'm not meant to sales No one can close these people That's the beginning and the end of this story. I don't like them doing because I don't think it's necessary But if they have to they can occasionally go down to 12 payments Right and people pay a little bit extra because they're spreading it out, but it makes it affordable to anyone, right? well It's very hard to imagine that someone couldn't come up with $300 a month thinking as someone who lives in the united states And myself who's in LA who's financially empowered, right? I'm not gonna tell me how great it is. How wonderful. This is amazing. Oh, sorry You can't have it You help nobody So start the the real point here of this training is because I'm seeing it happen in almost every company I work for Except not this is not the b2b. So if you're selling b2b, you don't really run into this much. But if you're selling b2c I'm saying that almost 75% of the time the primary objectives. I can't afford it. I and they really don't have the money Don't be scared to ask people About that financial picture now remember one of the core rules of Straight line prospecting Is you don't start off By asking invasive questions It's not going to be the first question. Don't get on the phone and say, okay It's a young guy. So how's the financial picture? Don't know start off with your big picture non-invasive questions first Be smart Very often from those big picture questions You'll find off. You'll you'll see the alarm bells go off You'll say, uh, oh, I might have someone I'm talking to that's not financially qualified And then you make sure you stop positioning your questions and deeing in more deeply If you get on the phone with someone and you immediately guys, oh, I owe my own business You're all clear You got the all clear sign man. You don't need to ask any questions about financial picture. Okay All right next question comes in from victor afratea What are some examples of a low action threshold if you cannot reduce the order size lowering the action threshold if you can't reduce the order side There's a lot well lowering if you can't reduce the order size well reducing the order size Doesn't lower someone's action threshold Okay, it just um it changes the energy in benefits out equation In other words when you lower the order size you're asking to make a smaller commitment, right? So their energy expenditure versus what what benefits are they getting? Right, and you're letting them essentially find out if it really is going to be that way Expending less energy money is still energy, right? Lowering action threshold. Let me explain to you what this is. It's a good question by the way Imagine a scale let me draw it out for you get easiest way Guys make sure you watch watch whiteboard wednesday on youtube These are all your negatives reasons not to buy these are all your positives reasons to buy right positives negatives As you move someone down the straight line from the open to the close right? Everything you say everything you do is designed to how you're knocking out negatives You're building on positives knocking out negatives building on right and eventually the scale tips, right? So when you're running your loops Whenever you loop around you keep adding on positives and you're knocking out negatives right? Hopefully at the end you get almost all negatives as many enough negatives out So the scale tips in the beginning right that kind of balance But look here the fulcrum is in the middle When you lower someone's action threshold What you're doing is You are changing the way They process the buying decision their beliefs about buying in other words When you lower someone's action threshold it's like pushing the fulcrum over here So the negatives now what happens the negatives have a lot less Power they don't exert as much force when the fulcrum is over here One positive now equals three negatives My father made he rest in peace was this ultra tough nut the crack as a buyer right His beliefs were that my best decisions are made slowly I don't trust people in general Sales people are out to get me People like that That fulcrum is over here People like me i'm over here right to me Okay, that that that fulcrum is over here. I'm sorry my dad's fulcrum is over here My fulcrum is over here where I'm like, yeah, I can trust sales people my best decisions So for me a negative my fulcrum cl what happens a negative doesn't exert much force When we lower someone's action thresholds what we're doing is we're changing their beliefs about buying And managing risk and by doing that It causes each negative to not matter as much and causes each positive to matter more One example is when you say to someone, you know, john when you get them very close Say, what's the worst that can possibly happen? Let's say i'm wrong and x y and the product is not as good as I say and you lose $500 We get the cash in your guarantee Is that gonna put is that gonna hurt you really? It's not gonna really affect your life that way. No, will you cash your guarantee? Yeah, we'll part as friends right not gonna destroy your life. No, all right But on the upside here If i'm even half right And you do even nearly as well as all my other clients in this abuse this product What's gonna happen over the next three months then you start future pacing So what you're doing is you're showing him That like the risk reward ratio and you're changing his belief and as you do that his focus starts sliding That's one way another way is through the money back guarantee You get it and then it's something called offsetting language rounds.
